name: HTCondor Submit Job
summary: Generate safe HTCondor submit descriptions for common high-throughput job shapes.
description: This skill helps users create HTCondor submit description files for single jobs, many-task queues, and GPU smoke tests with conservative resource requests, explicit logs, clear placeholders, and safe notes about file transfer and site policy.

inputs.name: submit_description
inputs.description: HTCondor submit description file defining executable, arguments, logs, resource requests, transfers, and queue statement.

inputs.name: executable
inputs.description: Executable or script referenced by the submit description file.

inputs.name: resources
inputs.description: Requested CPUs, memory, disk, GPUs, queue count, and file transfer needs.

inputs.name: site_policy
inputs.description: Site-specific accounting group, container, filesystem, or transfer policy.

outputs.name: submit_file
outputs.description: A reviewable HTCondor submit description file.

outputs.name: cluster_id
outputs.description: The cluster or job id printed by condor_submit after submission.

outputs.name: condor_logs
outputs.description: Log, stdout, and stderr files with per-cluster and per-process paths.

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced artifacts.
Check that the starter submit description exists and is non-empty.
Run condor_submit -dry-run against a starter submit file if supported by the local HTCondor version, then submit a one-process smoke job after confirming site file-transfer policy.
